name: health-export-ai
description: Query your Apple Health data — exported by the Health Export AI iOS app to iCloud Drive — from any MCP agent. Local-first, read-only, zero dependencies.
long_description: A zero-dependency MCP server that exposes 190 Apple Health metrics (exported by the Health Export AI iOS app into an iCloud Drive folder) to any MCP-compatible agent — Claude Desktop, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw, Hermes. No bridge, no Docker, no HTTP receiver: the server reads the synced cache file directly over stdio. Tools: get_mcp_status, list_metrics, get_health_metrics, get_trends, compare_periods, get_structured_export, query_health_data. Get the companion iOS app on the App Store: https://apps.apple.com/app/id6784185201

tools.name: get_mcp_status
tools.description: Health check: source, metric/workout counts, latest data date. Call first.

tools.name: list_metrics
tools.description: Every available metric with unit, day count, date range.

tools.name: get_health_metrics
tools.description: Daily values for a metric over a date range, with an aggregate.

tools.name: get_trends
tools.description: Recent N-day window vs the prior N days — change, % change, direction.

tools.name: compare_periods
tools.description: A metric across two arbitrary date periods (A vs B).

tools.name: get_structured_export
tools.description: Clean JSON for chosen metrics/range for an agent's context.

tools.name: query_health_data
tools.description: Natural-language convenience query routed to structured results.

health_data_dir.title: Apple Health export folder
health_data_dir.description: The iCloud Drive folder the Health Export AI iOS app writes to (contains .health-cache.json). On a Mac this is typically ~/Library/Mobile Documents/iCloud~ai~healthexport~app/Documents.

pairing_secret.title: Pairing code (optional)
pairing_secret.description: If you turned on Agent pairing in the iOS app (Settings → Agent pairing), paste the code here. Leave blank if pairing is off.
